name: Hollow Creek Plumbing and Drain
hook: River-town plumbing shop. Wes Tolliver owns the truck. Honest work, fair prices, and a phone that is finally answered.
owner_note: Owned and operated by Wes Tolliver
owner_note_long: Owned and operated by Wes Tolliver since 2012. Fourteen years of plumbing the river towns without a callback.

chat_exchange.text: My kitchen pipe just burst. Water everywhere.
Shut the water off at the main valve first. I will get Wes out to you in forty-five minutes. One-eighty after-hours plus parts. Want me to book it?
Yes please book it.
Booked. Wes is leaving the shop now. I will text you his ETA so you can watch for the truck.

name: Third Avenue Heat and Drain
hook: Mid-size metro service shop. Marcus Beale runs the crew. Same-day HVAC and drain work that does not get rescheduled.
owner_note_long: Owned by Marcus Beale. Eleven years in the trade, no subcontracted work, no surprise fees.

chat_exchange.text: No hot water and I have two kids here.
Marcus can come tonight for the after-hours fee or nine tomorrow morning. Tank or tankless, do you know?
Tank. Forty gallon I think.
Got it. I will book Marcus for nine AM with a tank-replacement quote ready. He will text you when he is on the way.

name: Knotwood Brothers Plumbing
hook: Family shop, two brothers. Eli and Dean Knotwood built it together in 2004. Twenty-two years, same two phone numbers.
owner_note: Eli and Dean Knotwood, brothers and owners
owner_note_long: Eli and Dean Knotwood started this together out of their dad's garage in 2004. Still the only two names on the van.

chat_exchange.text: I think there is a leak behind my bedroom wall. I can hear dripping.
Turn the water off at the main. Eli or Dean can be out inside the hour. Seventy-five dollar diagnostic, waived if we do the repair.
Please send someone.
Eli is heading your way. I will text you his ETA and a confirmation with your address.

name: Anvil Creek HVAC
hook: Rhonda Sayles owns the shop. She worked HVAC for fifteen years before she started it. Nine years and counting on her own crew.
owner_note_long: Owned by Rhonda Sayles. Fifteen years in the trade before she started the shop in 2017. She still runs calls herself when the crew is slammed.

chat_exchange.text: AC just quit and it is ninety-five outside.
Priority call. Rhonda can be there inside two hours. One-fifty service call. Any elderly or young kids in the house? I can bump the priority.
Yes, my mom is eighty-one.
Bumping it now. Rhonda is leaving the shop. ETA in forty minutes. I will text you the moment the truck pulls out.

name: Northbridge Plumbing Co
hook: Northern-metro shop, built through two recessions. Troy Armitage owns it. Eighteen years of not-missing-the-call.
owner_note_long: Owned by Troy Armitage. Built the shop in 2008 through a recession, grew it through another. Eighteen years, three trucks, zero subcontracted jobs.

chat_exchange.text: Sump pump died in the storm, water is coming in fast.
Troy can be out in thirty. Two-twenty emergency dispatch plus the pump. Want me to book it?
Yes book it.
Booked. Troy is leaving now. I will text you the second the truck pulls out so you can watch for him.

name: Old Mill Road Heating and Air
hook: Rural-suburb shop on Old Mill Road. Dale Crowley started it in 1999. Twenty-seven winters, never a no-show.
owner_note_long: Owned by Dale Crowley. Started the shop in 1999 out of a barn on Old Mill Road. Twenty-seven winters, still the number neighbors pass around when something breaks.

chat_exchange.text: Furnace will not fire. It is twenty degrees in here.
Dale can be there inside the hour. One-twenty-five diagnostic, waived on the repair. What is the address?
1247 Old Mill Road.
Dale is heading out. ETA forty minutes. I will text you when the truck leaves the shop.
